content:
# Revelation Chapter 1 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Book:** Revelation | **Chapter:** 1

**Verse 1 — "revelation" vs. "unveiling":**
- EMET: "revelation" (with transliterated names: "Iesous Christos")
- LOGOS: "revelation"
- PNEUMA: "unveiling" with expansive paraphrase ("This is the unveiling of Jesus Christ -- a revelation God gave him")
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "revelation" as the established English equivalent of apokalypsis, following EMET and LOGOS. PNEUMA's "unveiling" is etymologically accurate but departs from convention. The simpler opening structure of LOGOS was preferred over PNEUMA's double-phrase rendering. Names rendered in standard English (Jesus Christ) following LOGOS and PNEUMA rather than EMET's transliterations.

**Verse 1 — "shortly/soon/in swiftness":**
- EMET: "in swiftness"
- LOGOS: "shortly"
- PNEUMA: "soon"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "soon" as the most natural English rendering of EN TACHEI while remaining faithful. "In swiftness" is hyper-literal; "shortly" is slightly archaic.

**Verse 1 — "servants/slaves":**
- EMET: "slaves"
- LOGOS: "servants"
- PNEUMA: "servants"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "servants" (2-1 majority). DOULOS can mean both; "servants" is more appropriate for the covenantal relationship in view while "slaves" captures the social reality. In this theological context, "servants" prevails.

**Verse 2 — "testified/bore witness/testifies":**
- EMET: "testified" (past tense)
- LOGOS: "bore witness" (past tense)
- PNEUMA: "testifies" (present tense: "who now testifies")
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "bore witness" following LOGOS. The Greek aorist EMARTYRESEN is past tense; PNEUMA's present tense departs from the source.

**Verse 3 — Structural expansion:**
- EMET: "Blessed [is] the [one] reading and the [ones] hearing"
- LOGOS: "Blessed is the one who reads aloud, and blessed are those who hear"
- PNEUMA: "Blessed is the one who reads these prophetic words aloud, and blessed are those who hear them and take to heart what is written here, for the time of fulfillment is near"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Followed LOGOS closely. PNEUMA's "take to heart" interprets TEROUNTES ("keeping/guarding") more freely than warranted; "the time of fulfillment is near" adds "fulfillment" not present in the Greek.

**Verse 4 — "churches" vs. "assemblies":**
- EMET: "assemblies"
- LOGOS: "churches"
- PNEUMA: "churches"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "churches" (2-1 majority). While EKKLESIAI literally means "assemblies," "churches" is the received English rendering for these specific communities.

**Verse 4 — "who is to come" vs. "who is coming":**
- EMET: "the [one who is] coming"
- LOGOS: "who is to come"
- PNEUMA: "who is coming"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "who is coming" — a present participle (HO ERCHOMENOS) best rendered as ongoing/imminent.

**Verse 5 — "freed" vs. "loosed":**
- EMET: "loosed"
- LOGOS: "freed"
- PNEUMA: "freed"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "freed" (2-1 majority). Both are valid for LYSANTI; "freed" communicates more directly in modern English.

**Verse 6 — "might" vs. "dominion":**
- EMET: "might" (TO KRATOS)
- LOGOS: "dominion"
- PNEUMA: "dominion"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "dominion" (2-1 majority). KRATOS conveys sovereign ruling power; "dominion" captures this better.

**Verse 6 — "into the ages" vs. "unto the ages" vs. "forever and ever":**
- EMET: "into the ages of the ages"
- LOGOS: "unto the ages of the ages"
- PNEUMA: "forever and ever"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "unto the ages of the ages" following LOGOS, preserving the distinctive Hebraic idiom.

**Verse 7 — "mourn/beat their breasts":**
- EMET: "will beat [their breasts] over him all the tribes of the earth"
- LOGOS: "all the tribes of the earth shall mourn over him"
- PNEUMA: "all the peoples of the earth will beat their chests in grief because of him"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted a hybrid: "all the tribes of the earth shall beat their breasts in grief because of him." KOPSONTAI denotes physical mourning gestures. "Tribes" (PHYLAI) preserved over PNEUMA's "peoples."

**Verse 7 — "Yes, amen" vs. "Even so. Amen" vs. "Yes indeed. Amen":**
- EMET: "Yes, amen"
- LOGOS: "Even so. Amen"
- PNEUMA: "Yes indeed. Amen"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "Yes indeed. Amen" for the emphatic double affirmation (NAI, AMEN).

**Verse 8 — Quotation formatting:**
- EMET: No quotation marks
- LOGOS: No quotation marks
- PNEUMA: Quotation marks around the divine speech
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted quotation marks following PNEUMA for clarity.

**Verse 9 — "fellow-partaker/fellow partaker/partner":**
- EMET: "fellow-partaker"
- LOGOS: "fellow partaker"
- PNEUMA: "partner"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "fellow partaker" following LOGOS. SYNKOINONOS specifically means one who shares in something together.

**Verse 9 — "I came to be" vs. "was":**
- EMET: "I came to be in the island"
- LOGOS: "was on the island"
- PNEUMA: "I was on the island"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "was on the island" (LOGOS/PNEUMA).

**Verse 10 — "in spirit/in the Spirit/caught up in the Spirit":**
- EMET: "in spirit" (lowercase, ambiguous)
- LOGOS: "in the Spirit" (capitalized, with article)
- PNEUMA: "caught up in the Spirit" (interpretive addition)
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "in the Spirit" following LOGOS.

**Verse 10 — "lordly day/the Lord's day/the Lord's Day":**
- EMET: "the lordly day"
- LOGOS: "the Lord's day"
- PNEUMA: "the Lord's Day"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "the Lord's Day" with capitalization as a technical term.

**Verse 10 — "great voice/tremendous voice":**
- EMET: "great voice"
- LOGOS: "great voice"
- PNEUMA: "tremendous voice"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "great voice" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 11 — "scroll/book":**
- EMET: "scroll"
- LOGOS: "book"
- PNEUMA: "scroll"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "scroll" (2-1 majority). BIBLION in the first century refers to a scroll.

**Verse 12 — "the voice which was speaking with me" vs. "who was speaking to me":**
- EMET/LOGOS: "the voice that/which was speaking with me"
- PNEUMA: "who was speaking to me"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "the voice that was speaking with me."

**Verse 13 — "breast/chest":**
- EMET: "breasts"
- LOGOS: "chest"
- PNEUMA: "chest"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "chest" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 13 — "belt/sash":**
- EMET: "belt"
- LOGOS: "sash"
- PNEUMA: "sash"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "sash" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 14 — "as a flame of fire" vs. "blazed like fire":**
- EMET: "as a flame of fire"
- LOGOS: "as a flame of fire"
- PNEUMA: "blazed like fire"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "as a flame of fire" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 15 — "fine brass/burnished bronze":**
- EMET: "fine brass"
- LOGOS: "burnished bronze"
- PNEUMA: "bronze refined" (paraphrase)
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "burnished bronze" following LOGOS.

**Verse 15 — "sound/roar":**
- EMET: "sound of many waters"
- LOGOS: "sound of many waters"
- PNEUMA: "thundered like the roar of many waters"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "the sound of many waters" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 16 — "shines in the power of it" vs. "shining in its full strength" vs. "blazing at full strength":**
- EMET: "as the sun shines in the power of it"
- LOGOS: "as the sun shining in its full strength"
- PNEUMA: "like the sun blazing at full strength"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "as the sun shining in its full strength" following LOGOS.

**Verse 17 — "Do not fear/Fear not/Do not be afraid":**
- EMET: "Do not fear"
- LOGOS: "Fear not"
- PNEUMA: "Do not be afraid"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "Fear not" following LOGOS for its solemn register.

**Verse 18 — "Hades" vs. "the grave":**
- EMET: "the Hades"
- LOGOS: "Hades"
- PNEUMA: "the grave"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "Hades" following LOGOS. PNEUMA's "the grave" flattens the cosmological meaning.

**Verse 18 — "living I am" vs. "I am living" vs. "I am alive":**
- EMET: "living I am into the ages of the ages"
- LOGOS: "I am living unto the ages of the ages"
- PNEUMA: "I am alive forever and ever"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "I am alive unto the ages of the ages" — combining PNEUMA's natural phrasing with LOGOS's preservation of the Hebraic temporal formula.

**Verse 19 — "are about to come to be" vs. "are about to come to pass" vs. "will happen":**
- EMET: "about to come to be after these things"
- LOGOS: "about to come to pass after these things"
- PNEUMA: "what will happen after this"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "about to come to pass after these things" following LOGOS.

**Verse 20 — "upon the right hand" vs. "in my right hand":**
- EMET: "upon the right [hand] of me"
- LOGOS: "in my right hand"
- PNEUMA: "in my right hand"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "in my right hand" (2-1 majority).

— SOPHIA
